content:
In fact, we've found that some excellent voice actors don't actually cost as much as you'd think. While less well-known voice actors obviously have lower appearance fees, the potential costs of coordinating with others, repeated recording sessions, and possibly even having to re-record everything because the desired effect wasn't achieved can end up being much more expensive.

Especially when... it comes to recording for PVs, the acting requirements are often very high.

thread_title: 前瞻：V2.4.0.X 挑战性调整 Preview: V2.4.0.X Challenge Adjustments

content:
Honestly, I'm really thankful you took your time and addressed this.

I think everyone agree that the normal mode nerf is pretty much warranted. That's why I wasn't opposed to it and ends up rushing through arc 1 before story mode got nerfed. I did it and had fun with it. 

But Hard Mode nerf was super uncalled for. I took my time with the game. I only play whenever I am in the mood for it. And so, by the time the patch arrive, I was only on HM chapter 6. I still remember how 6-9 HM felt. Imagine my surprise when suddenly I bulldozed through it. And just... Not have fun at all. It felt like I was being punished for not finishing the HM sooner. 

So I'm glad that you will revert the difficulty nerf for Hard Mode. I appreciate that.
thread_title: 前瞻：V2.4.0.X 挑战性调整 Preview: V2.4.0.X Challenge Adjustments

content:
I find the best way to put it is, the fights before the nerf were balanced with intention. Had mechanics, and intended levels of progression to beat them. They weren't good... But they were there.

It genuinely feels like this story nerf was just fully rushed. Seeing things like enemies having less hp in later chapters. It feels fully based on the Devs analytics on when players stop playing the game. Rather then on any gameplay related intention. It feels like genuinely 0 thought was put in to any story/gameplay related development in this balancing cycle.

I'm not happy. I have on-boarded over 6 of my friends into playing this game. Not mentioning other creators. And my viewers. Knowing now that going through the story they won't even experience anything close to the team building experience I had in the game.

I completely disagree with lights sentiment about the game becoming trivial being unavoidable in gacha/live service games. 

In gachas that mostly comes in forms of powercreep, new units often trivialise old content. We can see the same in morimens, via Kath, Pollux, soon gmurphy. Where their inbuilt passives/base damage absolutely break early parts of the game.  But that's fine. It's a players choice to break the game. To look for the path of least resistance. 

I'm genuinely awestruck to see how many of you are fine with the normal mode staying like this. So you have 0 core memories of overcoming the challenges in this game and feeling joy.

Or even being like "if only this boss had a smidgen less hp" and not... Whatever we have now.

thread_title: 前瞻：V2.4.0.X 幻梦深潜 Preview: V2.4.0.X Phantasmal Dive

content:
Regarding phantasmal dive, I believe bringing back the ranking per realm is one good way to offer a better chance for each kind of team to shine.

Additional points for using certain awakeners would have been very welcome if Faded Legacy awakeners stopped being limited and were completely part of standard awakeners, which isn't the case, and I assume is not the direction Morimens is considering. It would have been fine to offer 1 standard and 1 limited bonus points per realm in such a case.
Ultimately though, the top of the ranking is for whales entertainment anyway and not for newbies, so giving extra points for using certain units is the only way to provide creativity.
Some possible option is to provide points also for certain SSR wheels, SR wheels, and covenant, and have a "max total" extra points you can reach, offering enough rooms for those who don't have all the awakeners to still rank high if they're creative enough with the options, and prevent huge gap difference from whales who can tick everything and blast through.
Then again, this will require testing overtime and I hope the community will welcome weekly rules changes to tinker and find the optimal phantasmal dive experience.


Now for the story, I don't have access to internal data, which I believe is what the game based on to decide on lowering the difficulty on story, and I'm inclined to say this is likely a good decision for the time being, but in huge part because of the 200 Luminous Core reward that requires advancing in story.
Morimens fell in the trap it created itself with this kind of event but hopefully this is temporary. Though, I would agree that normal mode could welcome some adjustment, however it was unexpected to see hard mode getting nerfed, although I can see it making sense if the normal mode is way too easy, the gap could be too big for new players in hard mode.
I'll set aside the irony of some story challenges being now extremely hard to complete because enemies have lower HP than the required damage to inflict, but it's a matter of adjustment.
Now, I believe it's important that the game keeps its "intended experiences", and when I played the game, the story and hints had all the elements to make me mentally prepared that the Death Resistance will be part of the gameplay. Although I haven't tested, nor can I test properly because I can't lower my own awakeners to experience like a newbie (and can't be bothered to start a new account) but I feel like, based on feedback I've seen so far, that the story is way too easy, that maybe the Death Resistance mecanism isn't even used at all.
There is also the consideration of story impact when completing a difficult chapter, which I believe plenty of veteran players have nostalgia from, and I still have a vivid memory of chapter 5 boss fights, that was a sudden jump in difficulty that forced me to use gnosis, and I have fond memory of it. However the 7-8 boss fight of chapter 9 not so much. I am aware of the context the game was going through when this chapter 9 released, but perhaps you could consider rewriting some part of it and make it closer to the original design? (Assuming the original design was similar to all previous chapters, but it's my wild guess)

In my opinion, the only way to satisfy most of the people, both new and nostalgic of the difficulty, is to create an "easy mode", that would still count toward story completion for the 200 Luminous Core, while the normal mode remains the intended game experience it was designed to offer.
Making what I assume is currently "easy" the "normal mode" could be misleading for what the player has to expect for the rest of the game.

Then again, I can see there is an intention to add madness mode in story, and I assume the veteran players are eager to do story in a new high difficulty, but the nerf of normal and hard mode makes me think we'll find the "hard mode" difficulty in the "madness mode", meaning there won't be new things for the veteran players.

content:
Even if you alter maps to not allow direct access to boss, total turns taken is a very boring requirement. 

If you did want to implement such a restriction, I think it's better just to confine it to Final Boss fight. 

I would also suggest that every fight you do actually INCREASES your points, for example, 100/200/300 points based on difficulty of the fight. 

Another suggestion would be to lose some amount of points per relic you pick up, maybe first 3 are free, greater than 3 relics is -200 points each, greater than 6 relics is an additional -200 points, for -400 total for each relic bought past 6. 

I understand you obviously want people who spend more to have an advantage, but it's very disappointing that actually playing the stage is discouraged, especially after it's no longer a daily burden like it was. 

I like the idea of the "Recommended Awakeners", but I think team building will still be very stagnant and narrow when the only goal is complete every combat you are required to enter in a single turn.

thread_title: 前瞻：V2.4.0.X 幻梦深潜 Preview: V2.4.0.X Phantasmal Dive

content:
Our response is not intended to satisfy any specific individual, but to keep everyone informed of our current situation. Allow me to share some additional thoughts.

From what we have observed and understood, even many long-time players struggle to clear certain stages in Arc 1—even with multiple revives. This can stem from several factors: some haven’t discovered the optimal, balance-defying explosive team compositions; others lack sufficient time invested in character progression; and in some cases, there are simply differences in players’ skill and strategic expertise.

Despite repeated explanations, misunderstandings still persist. The reason for this difficulty adjustment is that we are trying to make the steep progression curve smoother and more reasonable. The initial steepness was largely a necessary compromise due to the limited content available in the game’s early stages.

In the past, this approach worked well given the planned final content scope. However, with Arc 2 and even Arc 3 on the horizon, maintaining the status quo would make it increasingly difficult for new players to access the latest content. This is clearly unreasonable—they would struggle to catch up with the newest updates and storylines, inevitably encountering spoilers from story-related events or feeling isolated from the community.

Currently, we have more new players than ever before. Therefore, this adjustment prioritizes helping these new players catch up to long-time players’ progress. We also have future plans to accommodate players with different difficulty preferences, though these will require significant development time, which is why we haven’t shared details yet. For example, allowing players to challenge the main story directly on Hard mode without first clearing Normal.
thread_title: 前瞻：V2.4.0.X 幻梦深潜 Preview: V2.4.0.X Phantasmal Dive

content:
Our player base is more diverse than ever: story enthusiasts, art lovers, hardcore challengers, tactical innovators, and pure PVP fans. This diversity is a positive sign for the game’s growth. Our core challenge lies in preserving the game’s essence while fostering an inclusive environment where all types of players can coexist. This is no easy task, and there’s a real risk of missteps.

Internally, we have thoroughly deliberated on this matter, including the commitments we want to make to Morimens players to clarify the game’s future direction. We hope to share more details with everyone as soon as we wrap up our current workload.
thread_title: 前瞻：V2.4.0.X 幻梦深潜 Preview: V2.4.0.X Phantasmal Dive

content:
In my mind. The idea of helping new players catch up by this method of making a part of the game a complete mindless easy chore like this.  Is a betrayal of the audiences perceived values of the game. That mainly being the unapologetic difficulty. There were many other methods on board. 

To balance the stages to a point where most players could still have a challenge but clear it deathless would have been ideal.

More 1 time upgrade material rewards for people starting, would have had a similar effect of helping people catch up. FGO does it.

Now it leaves the story stages feeling completely dry.

thread_title: 前瞻：V2.4.0.X 幻梦深潜 Preview: V2.4.0.X Phantasmal Dive

content:
I see 3 interesting points to talk about

1. The legacy of chapters being the only hard endgame content until there were more content.
This is definitely part of reason the game need to adjust chapters difficulty overtime to smooth out the game experience, as the hard endgame story content is pushed further and further. I can only agree with this, and something we can observe in JP server, where their endgame chapter is much harder than our first chapters, but that's because they don't have yet a lot of content. (Putting aside the differences between our servers, and I haven't followed what's happening in the JP server for a while though, but basing this only on launch day information I have)

That said, I think chapter 9 has the heaviest legacy (for a legitimate reason due to context at the time) that makes transitioning into arc 2 really exhausting, that I'm not sure just changing numbers would be ideal, but redesigning isn't an easy option either.

2. Difficulty for new players to discover or figure out synergies or strong combos to overcome challenges.
I have been thinking for a while now, but I believe having a puzzle-like challenges that have existed in various card games where you must beat the level within the single turn with the cards and situation available to you is one good way to help new players to learn about strong combos they can have between characters. The character trial is first step to grasp the idea of the char mechanic, but this kind of puzzles should help further understand a lot of subtlety the game have, that they would otherwise only find out by messing around a lot, which is time consuming and not everyone have the patience for that.

3. Difficulty to clear the game overall for some players.
There is something I've always felt mixed about this game, and for the sake of clarity I'll exaggerate a bit, but I've always felt that part of the reason the game can prove to be difficult is simply because the standards characters are just awful. It always irked me a lot seeing how heavily reliant the Standards Characters are on Limited Characters to shine. I halfway forgive this design because the game is above average on pulling rate and generosity compared to all gachas. I really hope that the game consider adding the old limited into a full standard character pool, the same way Arknights does (with exception), and I still love Arknights for that. You may still keep Genesis Helot as exception and forever limited if needs be, but ultimately the current standard character pool isn't good enough to clear the story with a reasonable investment, you have to use limited awakeners to have a correct game experience the game is supposed to provide.
That said, I understand that the game need to sell, but as the game advance and there's more limited units to wait for rerun as a newbie, I feel like there will be a tipping point where this can't keep going like this.
Another point, still regarding balance, but some char design have been clearly thought with Over Exalt to function properly, which requires a lot of pulls. I spent a lot of money and I still dislike how the awakeners are designed through enlightenments, sometime an important part of mechanic is buried in there, making explaining how to play the character an actual nightmare and definitely doesn't help the game to be understood well. Kath being "trash" at E0 and "Goddess" at E3 is really annoying.
Once again, I'll take Arknights as an example, but their enlightenments are mostly stats buff and a few bit cost reduction, the char remains fully playable once acquired, which makes team building much easier. But in Morimens, you sometime must wait till certain enlightenments have been reached for a combo to work, further pushing the problem of how difficult the game is to understand and find combos.
thread_title: 前瞻：V2.4.0.X 幻梦深潜 Preview: V2.4.0.X Phantasmal Dive

content:
2. i'm one of the people who find these puzzles difficult and irksome, I enjoy finding combo's and solutions myself, rather then being sat trhough a compilation of how to play.

3rd. I mostly disagree. Morimens for a gacha, is already leagues above in normal banner character usability. Hell and even viability to be used as main parts of team with high investment as we see a lot in cn, Teams of standard banner characters carried by covenants and clever strats/wheel usage. Even without such commitment a lot of teams have normal banner characters as cheap cornerstones. 
Albeit this is just an another opinion that's going to be different from player to player, and how we choose to play the game. 
adding limiteds to the normal characeter roster would be fineee. 
Again, i am not a person who looks for the path of least resistance in building a unit, So a lot of the dupe complaints never hit me. Use what i have, work on what i want. Each unit upgraded can fit like 3-4 teams, and each unit i change out, can still work in D-tide.
